questions.question_text: The Utah Real Estate Purchase Contract (REPC) is approved by which entities?
questions.correct_answer: The Real Estate Commission and Attorney General
distractors: The Division of Real Estate and legislature
The Association of REALTORS only
The title company and lender
questions.explanation: The REPC is a standardized form approved by the Utah Real Estate Commission and the Office of the Attorney General. It is the standard purchase agreement used in Utah real estate transactions.

questions.question_text: A sales agent is helping a buyer complete the REPC. The agent may:
questions.correct_answer: Fill in the blanks on the approved form
distractors: Draft custom terms on a separate page
Modify the printed language of the form
Advise on the legal effect of each clause
questions.explanation: Licensees are authorized to fill in the blanks of approved forms like the REPC. They may not draft custom provisions, modify standard language, or provide legal advice about the effect of terms.

questions.question_text: A buyer asks their agent to add a special clause to the REPC requiring the seller to replace the roof. The agent should:
questions.correct_answer: Recommend the buyer consult an attorney
distractors: Draft the clause and add it to the contract
Add it as a handwritten note on the REPC
Tell the buyer it cannot be done
questions.explanation: Drafting custom contract provisions is the unauthorized practice of law. The agent should recommend the buyer consult an attorney to draft the special clause, which can then be added as an addendum.

questions.question_text: The REPC is considered a standardized form because it:
questions.correct_answer: Uses pre-printed language with blanks to fill in
distractors: Is identical to forms in all other states
Cannot be modified by any party
Must be signed by a government official
questions.explanation: The REPC is a standardized form with pre-printed approved language and specific blanks that licensees fill in with transaction details. It is unique to Utah and is periodically updated.

questions.question_text: A licensee uses a REPC version from two years ago that has since been updated. Using the outdated form is:
questions.correct_answer: Improper; current versions must be used
distractors: Acceptable if the client prefers it
Fine as long as the key terms are the same
Only problematic for commercial transactions
questions.explanation: Approved forms are periodically updated and licensees must use current versions. Using an outdated version could miss important legal changes and may expose parties to risk.

questions.question_text: Besides the REPC, which other documents are approved for use in Utah real estate transactions?
questions.correct_answer: Financing addenda, inspection addenda, counter offers
distractors: Only the REPC; no other forms are approved
Only listing agreements and buyer contracts
Only title company closing documents
questions.explanation: Various addenda and supplementary forms are approved for use including financing addenda, inspection addenda, and counter offers. All must be current approved versions.

questions.question_text: A buyer asks their agent to explain what the inspection contingency addendum does. The agent may:
distractors: Advise on whether to waive the contingency
Provide a legal interpretation of each clause
Recommend specific inspection companies only
questions.explanation: Licensees may explain the general purpose of approved forms. They may not provide legal advice about the effect of specific terms or advise whether to exercise or waive contractual rights.

questions.question_text: A client asks their agent whether signing the counter offer will create a binding contract. The agent should:
questions.correct_answer: Advise the client to consult an attorney
distractors: Confirm that signing creates a binding contract
Explain the legal implications in detail
Sign on the client's behalf to save time
questions.explanation: Questions about whether specific actions create legal obligations require legal analysis. Licensees must recommend attorney consultation for legal questions rather than providing their own legal interpretation.

questions.question_text: A licensee notices that an approved form has recently been updated. They should:
questions.correct_answer: Begin using the current version immediately
distractors: Continue using the old version until supplies run out
Wait for the Division to send a formal notice
Use whichever version the client prefers
questions.explanation: Licensees must use current versions of approved forms. When updates are released, the new version should be adopted immediately to ensure compliance with current legal requirements.

questions.question_text: Which best describes a licensee's rights when filling out approved real estate forms?
questions.correct_answer: Fill blanks and explain the general purpose
distractors: Draft supplementary clauses as needed
Interpret legal consequences for clients
Modify standard language for each transaction
questions.explanation: Licensees may fill in blanks on approved forms and explain their general purpose. They cannot draft custom provisions, interpret legal consequences, or modify the standard printed language.

questions.question_text: A sales agent drafts a deed for a client's property transfer. This is an example of:
distractors: Standard real estate practice
An allowed service for premium clients
Proper licensee conduct
questions.explanation: Drafting legal instruments like deeds from scratch constitutes the unauthorized practice of law. Only attorneys may draft such documents. Licensees are limited to filling in blanks on approved forms.

questions.question_text: A client asks their agent whether a specific contract term could make them liable for damages. How should the agent respond?
distractors: Provide their professional legal analysis
Research the answer and report back
Say they cannot discuss contract terms
questions.explanation: Advising on the legal effect of specific contract terms crosses into legal practice. The agent should recommend attorney consultation for questions about legal liability and contract interpretation.

questions.question_text: Which activity crosses the line from real estate practice into unauthorized practice of law?
questions.correct_answer: Advising on the legal effect of contract terms
distractors: Filling in the purchase price on the REPC
Explaining the general purpose of an addendum
Recommending a client get a home inspection
questions.explanation: Advising clients on the legal effect of specific contract terms constitutes UPL. Filling in blanks, explaining general purposes of forms, and making practical recommendations are within licensee scope.

questions.question_text: An agent writes a contract provision from scratch to address a unique situation in a transaction. The agent has:
distractors: Provided excellent customer service
Acted within their licensee authority
Made a minor procedural error
questions.explanation: Drafting contracts or custom provisions from scratch is the unauthorized practice of law. Licensees can only fill in blanks on forms approved by the Real Estate Commission and Attorney General.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent explains that the REPC contains an inspection contingency period. This explanation is:
distractors: Unauthorized practice of law
Only permitted with broker approval
Allowed only for experienced agents
questions.explanation: Explaining the general purpose and provisions of approved forms is within a licensee's scope. The line is crossed when the agent advises on the legal effect or consequences of those provisions.
